comments.post_body:
Over the last few months, I’ve been on a creative detour — moving from corporate to consulting, polishing my upcoming book, and experimenting with AI tools.

The result? Something I never imagined doing — creating music using AI! 🎶

Presenting “Udaipur Shahar-e-Noor”, a heartfelt AI-generated song celebrating the timeless beauty of Udaipur — the City of Lakes.

🌟 Made entirely with AI, with no background in music — just curiosity, creativity, and a love for my hometown.

As the world debates the ethics, safety and ownership of AI-generated art, I see it as a tool that empowers imagination.

🎧 Watch here  Short (10 sec) 👉 https://lnkd.in/eqcETKxN
 
Full Song (3 min) 👉 https://lnkd.in/eVHZwtGZ

If this resonates, please like, comment, and share it with your friends who love music, technology, or Udaipur.

#Udaipur #AI #Music #Innovation #Creativity #ArtificialIntelligence #AIMusic #CityOfLakes #TechMeetsArt
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about creating music using AI, where the author shares their AI-generated song 'Udaipur Shahar-e-Noor' and discusses the potential of AI as a tool for empowering imagination. The post celebrates the intersection of technology and art, and the commenter is likely reacting to this innovative use of AI in music creation.

comments.post_body:
I've retired from Intel!!  

Today I step away from what has been my home for the last 26 years, 8 months, and 22 days.  

Pictured is the very first product I ever worked on for Intel - The PCMCIA/CardBus Ethernet products, and my last Ethernet product - the Intel(r) Ethernet Controller E610 (pictured as an add-in card form factor, though I owned the silicon).  In between these two products I've been part of most of the Ethernet technology transitions - Copper Gigabit, 10GBASE-T, NBASE-T, 25Gb, RDMA, 100Gb, 200Gb, Time Sensitive Networking (TSN)... the list goes on.

However - I'll miss the people most of all.  They have been my friends and colleagues and have had an immeasurable impact on who I am today.  Though I will not be part of the team moving forward, I am very excited about the products coming out of the team in the future.

I'll be taking a small break before my next adventure.  Stay Tuned!!
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the author on their retirement from Intel after 26 years, and is likely responding to the author's nostalgic post about their time at the company and their accomplishments in the field of Ethernet technology. The author is reflecting on their career and expressing excitement for their next adventure.

comments.post_body:
This blog presents our benchmarking results for NVIDIA DGX Cloud workloads on Azure, scaling from 8 to 1024 H100 GPUs. We detail the Slurm-based setup using Azure CycleCloud Workspace for Slurm, performance validation via NCCL and thermal screening, and tuning strategies that deliver near-parity with NVIDIA DGX reference metrics.

Read the full blog:
https://lnkd.in/eEXKHmBv 

Huge thanks to the Azure + NVIDIA team that made this happen!

Azure HPC/AI: Jer-Ming Chia, Xavier Pillons, Ben Watrous, Ryan Hamel, Aditi Gaur, Andy Howard, Ojasvi Bhalerao, Hugo Affaticati, Michael Ringenburg, Kanchan Mehrotra
Azure Storage: Brian L., Dilip S. (now at Pure Storage)
Azure IB Interconnect: Remi Lemarchand, Jeff Yang, Jithin Jose, Austin Farmer, Chloe Gura, Rafael Salas, Jie Zhang, Nrupal Jani
NVIDIA SMEs: Alex Filby, Mark Arnold, Miro Enev, PhD, Zachary Newell, Nishanth Dandapanthula, Sowmyan Soman, Brian Carpenter, Emily Potyraj, Nishant Sharma

#AzureHPC #DGXCloud #LLM #NVIDIA #CycleCloud #Slurm #NDv5 #AIinfrastructure
comments.context_summary: The post discusses benchmarking results for NVIDIA DGX Cloud workloads on Azure, detailing the setup and performance validation, and thanks the Azure and NVIDIA teams for their collaboration. The commenter is responding to the post, likely to show appreciation for the benchmarking results or the collaboration between the two teams.

comments.post_body:
I walked out of Intel Jones Farm campus earlier today with mixed feelings. Like many other folks, I opted into getting the Enhanced Retirement Package. Today, 9/30/2024, is my last day at Intel Corporation.

I started working at Intel 29 years ago. It was an incredible journey. I had the privilege of knowing, meeting, learning from, and working with amazing and talented folks at Intel.

Thank you, Intel!

I look forward to embarking on a new endeavor.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is sharing their bittersweet feelings about leaving Intel after 29 years, having opted for the Enhanced Retirement Package, and is now looking forward to a new endeavor. They express gratitude to Intel for the incredible journey and opportunities they had while working there.

comments.post_body: I too have decided to leave Intel and embrace my next phase in my life's journey.  Joining Intel's' Communication Group in the early days during the dot.com era and throughout the following 24 years has provided great experiences in driving business for various connectivity products & technologies in all the market segments which Intel services. These include, Ethernet (controllers, switches), WiFi, Thunderbolt, vPRO, RDMA, and many more.  It has always been about the challenges of new markets, turning opportunities into wins and mostly the people along the way that has kept me at Intel over the years.  Thanks to many who have made it a wonderful experience.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is announcing their decision to leave Intel after 24 years, reflecting on their experiences and the people they've met along the way. They're expressing gratitude for the opportunities and relationships they've had during their time at the company.

comments.post_body:
My last day at Intel after 31 years (!!) was August 2nd. What a wild and wonderful ride! I'm feeling a bit bittersweet, but looking forward to my next job, starting in a couple of weeks (stay tuned!).

Throughout my career at Intel, I've had the opportunity to wear many hats. While my primary focus was on networking drivers, my journey took a few interesting detours along the way. Working for a big company always felt comfortable to me, and I cherished being part of a team that changed the world through technology. My niche was networking—primarily ethernet, and eventually, a lot of Linux.

Though I enjoyed the stability and the impact of working at a large company, some of my most memorable moments came from working on smaller, more focused teams. That sense of close-knit collaboration and singular focus is something I'm eager to experience again in my next chapter.

I've met countless incredible people at Intel and throughout the industry. I am deeply grateful for the lessons (both good and bad!) that they've taught me. Our lives are made from our experiences, and I'm thankful for all of them—though some I might not choose to repeat! 😄

Thank you, Intel, for an unforgettable 31 years. I suspect I'll always have blue blood from working so long at Intel! Here's to new adventures and myself finally taking that leap I've watched so many colleagues take! #ThankYou #NewBeginnings #Networking
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about the author's last day at Intel after 31 years, where they reflect on their career and express gratitude for the experiences and people they've encountered. The author is now looking forward to their next job and new adventures, marking a significant new chapter in their life.

comments.post_body: I am very proud to have had the opportunity to be a part of an Area Manager panel at our most recent National Business Conference in Nashville. Not only was I able to meet and conversate with our new CEO on stage but I did so in front of ~500 fellow teammates from different positions within the organization. While my role in this event was a minor one, it made me majorly proud. I am confident in our direction and our leadership. Excited for 2018 doesn't even begin to describe it. There are positions available all over the country - what are you waiting for? #BeBridgestone Brittany Marsh Truman Hyde Sean Espinosa
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about a National Business Conference, where the author was part of an Area Manager panel and met the new CEO. The author expressed pride and confidence in the company's direction and leadership, and is now encouraging others to join the organization.
